fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Massachusetts Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Massachusetts - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity massachusetts, how to transfer pharmacist license to massachusetts, pharmacist compact massachusetts, massachusetts pharmacist endorsement application

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Massachusetts.

steps.title: Create an ePLACE Portal Account
steps.description: Register for an account on the Massachusetts ePLACE Portal if you do not already have one. This is required to access and submit all license applications.

steps.title: Initiate Your Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your ePLACE Portal account, select 'File an Application,' then choose 'Board of Registration in Pharmacy,' and specifically the 'Pharmacist Initial License' application type (applicable for both examination and endorsement paths).

steps.title: Complete Application & Submit Documents
steps.description: Fill out the online application form completely, upload all required supporting documentation (e.g., pharmacy degree, internship hours, photo), and pay the required application fee via the ePLACE Portal.

steps.title: Pass Required Examinations
steps.description: Register for and pass the North American Pharmacist Licensure Examination (NAPLEX) and the Massachusetts Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE). Endorsement applicants typically only need to pass the MA MPJE.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow instructions provided by the Board to complete a mandatory fingerprint-based criminal background check through approved state vendors.

steps.title: Verify Out-of-State Licenses (Endorsement Only)
steps.description: If applying by endorsement, arrange for verification of all professional licenses held in other jurisdictions to be sent directly to the Massachusetts Board of Registration in Pharmacy, often facilitated through the NABP's Electronic Licensure Transfer Program (ELTP) or other official means.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Massachusetts pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Massachusetts have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Massachusetts. To work in Massachusetts, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Massachusetts?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Massachusetts while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Massachusetts
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in Massachusetts, all applications, whether by examination or endorsement, must be submitted online through the state's ePLACE Portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Create an ePLACE account by clicking 'Register for an Account' on the portal homepage if you are a new user. Once registered and logged in, select 'File an Application' and choose 'Board of Registration in Pharmacy,' then select the appropriate Pharmacist Initial License application type.
